pathways: GA biosynthesis early-pathway: CPS -> KS -> KO -> KAO -> GA12/GA53 (shared diterpenoid arm)
GA biosynthesis late-pathway: TaGA20ox -> GA20/GA9 -> TaGA3ox -> bioactive GA1/GA4
GA catabolism: TaGA2ox (paralogs A9/A13/A3 families) -> inactive GA8/GA34/GA110
GA perception: Gibberellin + TaGID1 -> DELLA -> SCF-SLY1 -> 26S proteasome degradation
DELLA (Rht series): Rht-A1, Rht-B1, Rht-D1 homeologs; dwarfing alleles Rht-B1b/c/d/e/i and Rht-D1b/c
Other Rht non-DELLA loci: Rht8 (TaRNHL-D1), Rht12 (TaGA2oxA13), Rht13 (NB-LRR), Rht18/24 (TaGA2ox-A9), Rht25 (PLATZ-A1), Rht4/5 (uncloned)
BR biosynthesis: TaD11 (C6-oxidase), TaDWF4 (CYP90B rate-limiting), TaBRD2, TaDET2
BR perception/signalling: Brassinosteroid + TaBRI1 + TaSERK/BAK1 -> BSK/CDG/BSU1 -> TaBIN2 -> TaBZR1/TaBES1
Auxin: TaYUCCA biosynthesis, TaTIR1 perception, TaIAA, TaARF, TaGH3 conjugation, TaPIN1 transport
Cell-wall elongation: Expansins (TaEXPA/B), XTHs downstream of GA/BR/auxin
Light / phytochrome: Phytochrome -> PIF sequestration by DELLA; TaERF-A1 -> TaPIF4 activation
Nitrogen-DELLA crosstalk: nitrate -> GA metabolism -> DELLA destabilisation
canonical_mutants: Treatments: GA3, PAC, Uniconazole, BL (24-epiBL), Brassinazole, NAA, high/low nitrate
Trap-3 signalling-mutant rescues: Rht-B1b + GA3 (expect unchanged-to-moderate decrease), Rht-B1c + GA3 (unchanged), TaBRI1 triple + BL (unchanged decrease)
crosstalk: DELLA -> GA biosynthesis feedback (DELLA activates GA20ox transcription)
BR -> DELLA (BZR1-DELLA physical interaction; BIN2 phosphorylates DELLA)
Auxin -> GA20ox (induces GA biosynthesis in elongating stems)

gaps.pathway: GA biosynthesis early-pathway (CPS, KS, KO, KAO)
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 curated TaGA20ox, TaGA3ox, TaGA2ox and TaGA1ox1 but not the upstream diterpenoid-cyclase/P450 enzymes (CPS, KS, KO, KAO). These enzymes supply GA12/GA53 to the TaGA20ox rate-limiting step and are cloned in wheat (Huang et al. 2012). Without them the BUILDER sees GA20ox as a source and cannot model pathway perturbation by CPS/KS/KO/KAO variants.
search_queries: wheat TaCPS TaKS TaKO TaKAO gibberellin early biosynthesis enzyme
papers_read: Huang et al. 2012 Funct Integr Genomics (10.1007/s10142-011-0243-2)

gaps.node: Rht loci mechanism edges (Rht18, Rht24, Rht12, novel TaOSCA1.4)
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 listed Rht18, Rht24, and Rht12 in perturbations (T019-T023) but did NOT wire them to TaGA2ox at the edge level. Published mechanism: Rht24b, Rht18, and Rht12b all upregulate TaGA2ox paralogs (A9, A9, A13 respectively) via promoter or cis-regulatory variation. Without these edges a KO of Rht24 cannot propagate through the network. TaOSCA1.4 is a novel Ca2+-channel Rht gene (Lv 2024) with no Step 1 coverage at all.
search_queries: Rht24b TaGA2ox-A9 wheat Tian 2022 mechanism expression plant height
Rht12b TaGA2oxA13 wheat dwarfing allele plant height mechanism
wheat reduced height gene TaOSCA1.4 2024 calcium channel
papers_read: Lv K et al. 2024 Front Plant Sci (10.3389/fpls.2024.1381243)
Ford BA et al. 2018 Plant Physiol (already in Step 1; re-read for mechanism quote)
Tian X et al. 2022 New Phytol (already in Step 1; re-read for mechanism quote)

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 included TaD11 (C6-oxidase, BR biosynthesis late step) but not TaDWF4 which is the rate-limiting branch-point (22a-hydroxylation of campesterol). Milner et al. 2022 show TaDWF4 OE increases wheat productivity 45-101% on marginal N, establishing TaDWF4 -> BR -> growth axis.
search_queries: wheat TaDWF4 CYP90B1 brassinosteroid biosynthesis plant height
papers_read: Milner MJ et al. 2022 Commun Biol (10.1038/s42003-022-03139-9)

gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 had TaBRI1 but no co-receptor. In the canonical model BAK1/SERK family heterodimerises with BRI1 to trigger kinase activation upon BR binding. TaBRI1 triple-KO phenotype is only interpretable with a co-receptor node.
search_queries: wheat TaBAK1 brassinosteroid coreceptor plant height SERK
papers_read: Singh A et al. 2016 PLoS ONE (10.1371/journal.pone.0153273)

gaps.biological_justification: Rht8 = TaRNHL-D1 is curated as source-only in Step 1. Upstream regulation of TaRNHL-D1 expression is not described in published wheat work; accepting as source is biologically justified.
gaps.unclosable_reason: No published upstream regulator of TaRNHL-D1 in wheat; Rht8 treated as near-source, which matches the biology.

gaps.biological_justification: TaTB1 (TEOSINTE BRANCHED 1) has review-level evidence only (Pearce 2021) and no curated wheat-specific upstream regulators. Low severity because it has only one out-edge and weak evidence already flagged in Step 1.
gaps.unclosable_reason: No published mechanistic wheat paper defining TaTB1 upstream regulation; better suited to REFINEMENT as literature_gap.

gaps.pathway: Auxin -> GA20ox (auxin promotes GA biosynthesis in elongating stems)
gaps.biological_justification: Auxin-induced GA biosynthesis is an established cross-talk (ARF/IAA + AuxRE in GA20ox promoter, primary papers in Arabidopsis/tobacco/bamboo). No published wheat primary paper found with OA full-text quantifying this edge. Reviewed in auxin-hormone crosstalk literature but not mechanistically in wheat.
search_queries: auxin induces GA20ox biosynthesis stem elongation wheat crosstalk
gaps.unclosable_reason: No primary wheat paper with OA access; Arabidopsis/bamboo mechanism not extrapolated to wheat in current literature. Residual gap for BUILDER.

gaps.pathway: TaERF-A1 -> TaPIF4 transcriptional activation; TaPIF4 regulation of height
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 curated DELLA -> PIF (sequestration) but not the upstream activator of TaPIF4. Li et al. 2024 Plant Communications establishes TaERF-A1 as direct transcriptional activator of TaPIF4 with EMSA/ChIP evidence; JD6 natural variant produces semi-dwarfing. This closes the PIF activation half of the light-elongation loop and adds a new, paper-worthy height hub.
search_queries: wheat TaPIF PHYTOCHROME INTERACTING FACTOR plant height homolog Triticum
wheat TaERF-A1 ERF dwarfing plant height DELLA 2024
papers_read: Li L et al. 2024 Plant Communications (10.1016/j.xplc.2024.101194)
Zhuang H et al. 2024 PLoS ONE (10.1371/journal.pone.0296269) - wheat PIF family census

gaps.biological_justification: Canonical BR/GA biosynthesis perturbation set was under-populated. TaDWF4 OE added via Milner 2022. TaGA20ox RNAi primary wheat paper (Appleford 2007) not retrieved via OA search; Uniconazole treatment has no dedicated wheat height publication with OA text. TaGA20ox RNAi residual.
search_queries: wheat TaGA20ox RNAi transgenic plant height Appleford 2007
wheat Uniconazole plant height GA biosynthesis
papers_read: Milner MJ et al. 2022 Commun Biol (10.1038/s42003-022-03139-9)
gaps.unclosable_reason: TaGA20ox RNAi (Appleford 2007 Plant Biotech J) is paywalled with no PMC OA; Uniconazole wheat paper not located with OA access. Partial closure via TaDWF4 OE.

gaps.pathway: Environmental inputs: Photoperiod (Ppd-D1), Nitrogen -> GA/DELLA axis
gaps.biological_justification: Step 1 captured Vernalization -> VRN1 but not Photoperiod -> Ppd-1 or Nitrogen -> GA/DELLA. Both are well-characterised: Chen et al. 2018 (Ppd-D1a reduces height 10.2% via internode shortening) and Camut et al. 2021 Current Biology (nitrate -> GA metabolism gene expression -> DELLA destabilisation).
search_queries: wheat Ppd-1 photoperiod plant height internode elongation mechanism
wheat sucrose nitrogen nitrate plant height internode GA
papers_read: Chen Z et al. 2018 Front Plant Sci (10.3389/fpls.2018.01312)
Camut L et al. 2021 Current Biology (10.1016/j.cub.2021.09.024)

gaps.description: Step 1 uses a mixed convention: DELLA is split by homeolog (Rht-B1, Rht-D1 are separate nodes) but GA-oxidases (TaGA20ox, TaGA3ox, TaGA2ox, TaGA1ox1), TaGID1, TaBRI1, VRN1 are composite. TaD11-2A and TaZnF-B are homeolog-specific (trailed by '-2A' / '-B' suffix).
gaps.biological_justification: The mixed convention is actually defensible: (a) DELLA alleles (Rht-B1b vs Rht-D1b) have distinct phenotypic magnitudes and are inherited on different chromosomes, so splitting B/D is biologically meaningful; (b) GA-oxidase homeologs are functionally redundant with overlapping expression, so composite treatment is standard; (c) TaD11-2A and TaZnF-B are specific alleles whose phenotype ties to one homeolog.
gaps.decision: RETAIN Step 1 convention: DELLA homeolog-specific (Rht-B1, Rht-D1); GA-pathway composite (TaGA20ox, TaGA3ox, TaGA2ox, TaGID1, TaBRI1, TaBZR1, VRN1); allele-specific where evidence is homeolog-specific (TaD11-2A, TaZnF-B, PLATZ-A1, TaERF-A1, TaBRI1-A1 missense in perturbations, TaGA1ox1-B1).

bidirectional_audit.resolution: Step 1.5 did not attempt to regulate existing source-only nodes upstream (the paper evidence on TaSLY1 upstream etc. is sparse in wheat and the Step 1 flagging already justifies source treatment). Several newly added nodes are biosynthesis enzymes or locus-level Rht variants that are intentionally modelled as sources (TaCPS/KS/KO/KAO are constitutively expressed; Rht allele variants are cis-regulatory changes). Net effect: richer source layer, appropriate for BUILDER's is_source flag handling.

extraction_density_audit.step_1_papers_with_only_1_2_edges: Approximately 12 of 31 (~39%) full-text-read papers yielded only 1-2 edges; the review papers (Pearce 2021, Wang 2025) and the BR pathway paper (Gill 2025) are near the 1-2 mark.
extraction_density_audit.comment: Re-read of Rht18/24/12 primary papers yielded the Rht-locus -> TaGA2ox mechanism edges (E068-E070) that Step 1 missed because extraction stopped at phenotype-level evidence. Other low-density papers (Pearce 2021 review, Wang 2025 review) were not re-read because they are survey articles where low per-paper density is expected.

homeolog_handling_decision.convention: DELLA split (Rht-B1, Rht-D1 separate); GA-pathway and BR-receptor genes composite (TaGA20ox, TaGA3ox, TaGA2ox, TaGID1, TaBRI1, TaBZR1, TaBIN2, VRN1, TaGRF4, TaARF, TaIAA, TaTIR1, TaEXP, Phytochrome_Interacting_Factor); explicit homeolog where the allele name is phenotype-linked (TaD11-2A, TaZnF-B, PLATZ-A1, TaARF4, TaGA1ox1-B1)
homeolog_handling_decision.consolidations_performed: None - Step 1 convention retained; new nodes (TaCPS, TaKS, TaKO, TaKAO, TaDWF4, TaSERK, TaERF-A1, TaOSCA1.4, Rht18, Rht24, Rht12, Ppd-D1) follow composite convention except Ppd-D1 which is homeolog-specific because Ppd-D1a allele is the published phenotype driver.
homeolog_handling_decision.rationale: DELLA homeolog split is biologically motivated (B1/D1 alleles on different chromosomes with distinct phenotypes); composite for biosynthesis enzymes reflects functional redundancy; homeolog-specific where the allele name IS the phenotype driver (common in breeding literature).

residual_gaps_for_builder: G007 - Auxin -> TaGA20ox crosstalk: established in Arabidopsis/bamboo but no OA wheat primary paper. BUILDER may include as literature_gap with Arabidopsis citation or omit.

G009 (partial) - TaGA20ox RNAi / OE primary wheat perturbation evidence paywalled (Appleford 2007 Plant Biotechnol J). Uniconazole treatment on wheat has no OA primary paper. Perturbation set limited accordingly.
G005 - Rht8 (TaRNHL-D1): no published upstream regulator. Keep as near-source.
G006 - TaTB1: review-level evidence only; no primary wheat paper for upstream regulation. Low-confidence edge already in Step 1.
Rht4, Rht5, Rht26 causative genes still uncloned (Step 1 already flagged in literature_gap_log).
TaTB1 downstream targets in wheat not curated (out-degree 1); BUILDER may omit or flag.

TaGRF1 is a sink-only node (IN=1 from PLATZ-A1, OUT=0). BUILDER should either (a) add TaGRF1 -> Plant_Height to avoid dead-end trap (Trap 4) or (b) omit TaGRF1 from the model and keep it only in curated_edges.
